notes: The traditional meaning of 2.36 is the rhetorical climax of the disgrace argument: enemies will speak indecent words while disparaging Arjuna's earned prowess; there is no greater pain than this. The engineering layer maps this onto the senior engineer's specific professional vulnerability (peers who already wished failure rewriting earned reputation in load-bearing forums). The stance is preserved — Krishna is NOT telling Arjuna 'fight because critics will mock you'; Krishna is layering a social cost onto a sound technical conclusion. The engineering layer preserves this through the falsifiability and counter-example, both of which explicitly anchor to Ch. 18.66's right-renunciation. There is no inversion; the verse's directional pressure (toward engagement, when the engagement is technically warranted) is preserved exactly. PASS.
